summary: Reviewed the five live-game fixes for assumptions around realtime state, polling cleanup, timing-safe setup auth, and live-stage validation. The setup-secret comparison and server-side live-stage checks are directionally sound, but the host state regression guard was removed in the exact place the prior code documented a heartbeat/realtime stale-state hazard. Polling cleanup exists, though the new fallback can still overlap requests under poor network conditions.
appears_sound: `src/app/api/setup/route.ts:14` hashes both provided and expected setup secrets before `timingSafeEqual`, avoiding variable-length comparison errors while preserving constant-length comparison.
`src/app/host/actions.ts:948` keeps `announceWin` behind `requireController` before checking live game state, so stage validation does not replace host authorization.
`src/app/host/actions.ts:1123` resolves `recordWinner` call count from the live `game_states` row instead of trusting the client-provided `callCountAtWin` value.

findings.description: The change removes the explicit guard that prevented realtime heartbeat or polling updates from regressing `called_numbers` and `numbers_called_count`. In a live cash bingo game, a stale update can invisibly remove the just-called number from the host UI and validation state.
findings.evidence: The previous guarded `setCurrentGameState` at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:391` was replaced with unconditional `setCurrentGameState(payload.new)`, and the polling path at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:433` now unconditionally applies `freshState`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If every heartbeat and polling read is guaranteed to return a row version with the latest called-number fields, the regression cannot occur.
findings.what_would_confirm: Replay the known heartbeat/update ordering scenario or inspect `sendHeartbeat` and `callNextNumber` writes to prove stale row payloads cannot arrive after a newer local called-number state.

findings.description: The new polling fallback schedules a new async poll every 3 seconds without preventing overlap, so slow Supabase responses can race and allow an older request to overwrite fresher session or game state. This is user-reachable on poor venue Wi-Fi, which the project notes as a real-time lag gotcha.
findings.evidence: `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:165` starts `setInterval(() => { void poll(); }, POLL_INTERVAL_MS)` and `poll` has no in-flight guard or request ordering check before state updates.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If Supabase responses consistently complete in under 3 seconds and arrive in request order, overlapping polls will not manifest.
findings.what_would_confirm: Throttle network latency above 3 seconds and verify whether older `sessions` or `game_states_public` responses can apply after newer realtime or polling updates.

findings.description: The player polling fallback has the same unguarded overlap assumption as the display UI, risking stale active-game, prize, or called-number state being applied after a newer update. For players, this can make the card screen briefly show the wrong live stage or number history during active play.
findings.evidence: `src/app/player/[sessionId]/player-ui.tsx:192` starts `setInterval(() => { void poll(); }, POLL_INTERVAL_MS)` while `poll` updates `currentSession`, `currentGameState`, and `currentPrizeText` without an in-flight or monotonic freshness check.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The existing realtime subscription may usually correct stale polling writes quickly enough that players do not notice.
findings.what_would_confirm: Run the player view with delayed polling responses and fast stage/number changes to see whether stale poll results can overwrite the latest realtime state.

empty_categories: No timing-safe comparison regression found in the setup route based on the pack: both digests are fixed-length before `timingSafeEqual`.
No missing server-side controller check found in the changed `announceWin` and `recordWinner` paths; both still call `requireController` before mutation logic.
No polling cleanup leak found in the changed player/display effects; both clear the interval and remove the visibility listener on cleanup.
